title: [Feature]: Evaluate bounded subject-aware observability policies
problem: The current observability policy avoids exporting subjects and other high-cardinality metadata by default. Some future operators may want per-subject insight, but subjects can reveal sensitive operational structure and can create cardinality problems. This needs a deliberate policy model rather than a simple switch.
proposal: Evaluate a bounded subject-aware metrics policy that allows explicitly approved subject families, redaction or hashing options if appropriate, cardinality caps, and clear refusal behavior when a policy would export too much detail. The default must remain no subject sharing.
users: Operators who need controlled insight into subject-family behavior, security reviewers, and maintainers responsible for observability connector safety.
delivery_semantics: Subject-aware metrics must not affect delivery semantics. Metrics suppression or export failure cannot change ACK behavior, sink writes, retries, or DLQ decisions.
security: Subjects may be sensitive. The design must treat them as controlled metadata, avoid default export, enforce allow lists and bounds, and document the information-sharing risk.
acceptance: A threat model for subject-aware metrics is documented.
Policy options are bounded and fail closed.
Tests cover allow, deny, cardinality cap, and invalid policy behavior if code is added.
Documentation explains when not to enable this feature.
tests: Add policy unit tests if implementation is included. For design-only work, run documentation checks and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/observability.md, docs/metrics.md, docs/security.md, ROBOTS.md, AGENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after release publication with either implementation or documented design decision. Include release tag, policy decision, tests, and limitations.
